Common Thread
Every concept leverages Westfield's core competency — engineering clever folding mechanisms into durable, mass-market products.
Next Steps
Prioritize prototyping pipeline. Systems Wagon and Suspend Chair are closest to validation. Instant Indoor needs sectional sample review and system roadmap clarity. Origami needs material testing.
01
Instant Indoor
A full indoor furniture system built from Westfield’s folding-platform expertise
Instant Indoor

A Full Indoor System — Starting with the Sectional

  • Sectional-first launch platform second-round China samples focus on the modular sectional as the proof point
  • System roadmap beyond sectionals side chairs, director chairs, tables, and lounges using the same fold-flat platform logic
  • Initial sectional variants: bench-seat and standard cushion layouts to validate comfort, fabric tailoring, and assembly experience
  • Expandable furniture family sectional → side chair → director chair → lounge → table family, all sharing a recognizable Instant Indoor language
$18.6B Flat-pack market (2024)
$600–$2.5K Target price range
4–20% Annual growth
Strategic Advantage
Non-seasonal revenue stream on a proven folding steel-tube platform. The sectional leads because it proves the highest-value room setting first; side chairs, director chairs, tables, and lounges can follow as a coordinated system. Fabric: Texstyle "Louis" bouclé — 100% SDP, 62,000 cycle abrasion, Off White & Kit colorways.
Instant Indoor — Next Round Development

Proposed Indoor ZGL

  • New system extension: padded indoor zero-gravity lounge direction for the next development round.
  • Design language: indoor upholstery over a clean tubular frame, borrowing the relaxed posture and adjustability of outdoor ZGL.
  • Development question: can this become the lounge expression of Instant Indoor alongside the sectional, side chairs, director chairs, and tables?
June China Review
Use this as a proposed direction to discuss with the factory after reviewing the second-round sectional samples.
02
Origami Wagon
A single-sheet polymer wagon body using living hinge technology
Origami Wagon

One Sheet. No Fabric. Folds to a Briefcase.

  • Single polymer sheet with living hinges replaces tube + fabric construction
  • Flat-folds into briefcase form — dramatically smaller than quad-fold wagons
  • Structure is the basket — no separate frame needed
  • Deploys elegantly, stores more compactly than any wagon on market
$1.42B Folding wagon market
$500M North America
0 Direct competitors
Category Creation Opportunity
No existing product uses living-hinge origami for a wagon body. First mover creates the category.
Origami Wagon — Competitive Landscape

Current Wagons: Same Construction, Different Labels

Every folding wagon on the market uses the same tube-and-fabric approach. Origami breaks the paradigm.

Mac Sports Quad-Fold
$90–$110
Standard quad-fold. Large folded size. Fabric wears over time.
Timber Ridge Bi-Fold
$100–$130
Bi-fold design. Still tube + fabric. Slightly more compact.
Radio Flyer 3-in-1
$120–$150
Brand heritage play. Fixed form, does not fold flat.
Veer Cruiser
$300–$400
Premium lifestyle wagon. Does not fold at all. Stroller-quality.
🟢 Origami Wagon
TBD
Only wagon with polymer living-hinge body. Flat-folds to briefcase. Zero fabric.
Key Risk
Full-scale mechanism durability is unproven. Living hinge fatigue and load capacity need validation through prototyping.
Origami Wagon — Development Update
📍 Current Status
One-third SRPP prototype reviewed; SRPP has been pushed as far as it makes sense for now. Next plan shifts toward a molded-plastic sample.
Prototype Learnings
  • ▸Accordion body also folds through the bottom, creating an uneven load-bearing surface.
  • ▸Top edge collapses inward without reinforcement.
  • ▸50 kg load target drives thickness, stiffness, fold radius, and folded volume.
Next Development Direction
  • ▸Center next sample plan around molded plastic rather than continuing SRPP as the main body.
  • ▸Evaluate Guzzini-style storage construction as a reference for molded living-hinge behavior.
  • ▸Clarify target use case and load requirement before committing to full-scale construction.
03
Systems Wagon
Modular transport platform built on the milk-crate ecosystem with M-LOK mounting
Systems Wagon

One Platform. Infinite Configurations.

  • 24-quart milk crate ecosystem — 28″W × 40″L fits 4 crates in 2×2 grid
  • M-LOK mounting system for tool-free accessory attachment
  • Razors-and-blades model — base chassis + high-margin accessories
  • Base configurations target sideline, camping, and tactical use cases from the same modular chassis
$180–$250 Base chassis
50–70% Accessory margins
$450–$750 Customer LTV
NOT a Folding Wagon
Competes with them, but purpose-built for organized utility. Rigidity is the feature, not a trade-off.
Systems Wagon — Development Update
📍 Development Status
Concept engineering — M-LOK platform design with factory
Key Progress
  • ▸Key pivot: NOT a folding wagon — purpose-built modular platform
  • ▸24-quart milk crate ecosystem with M-LOK mounting system defined
  • ▸Veer competitive analysis and accessory ecosystem analysis complete (Feb 12)
  • ▸Target interior: 28"W × 40"L (fits 4 crates in 2×2 grid)
Engineering Learnings
  • ▸M-LOK (20mm spacing, T-slot nuts) provides proven modular mounting
  • ▸Frame options narrowed: aluminum extrusion, stamped steel, or injection molded
  • ▸Three distinct markets: outdoor rec, urban utility, commercial/trade
Risks & Open Questions
  • ▸M-LOK licensing/compatibility needs verification for consumer products
  • ▸Weight vs. modularity tradeoff — more mounting = heavier platform
  • ▸Price target needs validation against folding wagon category expectations
Next Milestones
  • ▸Factory concept updates on frame construction ~March 20
  • ▸April factory visit: review frame prototypes and M-LOK integration
  • ▸Accessory ecosystem MVP definition
04
Suspend Chair
A folding camp chair with a suspended seat — bounce, sway, and rock in one motion
Suspend Chair

Portable Comfort with Suspended Motion

  • Suspended seat with elastic + static webbing creates bounce/sway motion
  • Portable frame turns suspended comfort into a camp-ready chair format
  • Lia's 3D-printed model proves the concept — cord suspension works
  • June China review will validate the half-scale model, CAD direction, and path to sample
Suspend Chair — Development Update
📍 Development Status
Half-scale model and CAD brought to China; looking forward to reviewing the chair in June
Key Progress
  • ▸Folding camp chair with suspended webbing seat for multi-axis motion
  • ▸Half-scale model completed and brought to China for review
  • ▸CAD package brought to China alongside the physical model
  • ▸June review will define the next sample direction
Engineering Learnings
  • ▸Static + elastic webbing creates tilt/sway/rock without complex mechanism
  • ▸Portable folding frame remains the core differentiator
  • ▸Target is a simple, credible suspended-motion chair that can be sampled cleanly
Risks & Open Questions
  • ▸Webbing tension calibration critical — too loose = unstable, too tight = no motion
  • ▸Folding mechanism must not compromise the suspended seat geometry
  • ▸Weight capacity with suspended seat needs extensive testing
Next Milestones
  • ▸June China review: evaluate half-scale model, CAD, and mechanism direction
  • ▸Confirm next-round sample requirements with the factory
  • ▸Define webbing tension, frame geometry, and load-test plan after review
05
GRAM Chair
Compliant mechanism rocking — lightweight parallelogram motion on a director's chair platform
SKW · GRAM Chair

Director's Chair Meets Compliant Mechanism

  • Built on a standard director's chair frame — proven, cost-effective platform
  • Elastomeric corner joints (shown in red) replace rigid hardware at all four corners
  • Under weight shift, corners flex — frame transitions from square to parallelogram
  • The geometry change itself creates the rocking arc
  • No runners, springs, or linkages — just material flex
  • Each corner joint = pivot + spring + stop in one component
The Name
GRAM — dual reference to lightweight construction (lighter than any existing rocking solution) and the parallelogram motion.
GRAM — Competitive Landscape

Every Competitor Adds Parts. GRAM Redesigns the Joints.

ProductMechanismAdded WeightAdded PartsFold Impact
GCI FreestyleSpring-loaded ski runnersHigh (~12 lbs total)Runners + springs + bracketsBulky — runners protrude
GCI KICKBACKFulcrum + coil spring + runnerHighFulcrum + spring sleeve + skiModerate
HFDT Roll-TubeCurved roll tubes (runners)ModerateRoll tubes + guiding membersModerate
Westfield FlowMotionCantilever spring + separate baseModerateLeaf springs + base assemblyRequires separate base
🟢 GRAM ChairCompliant elastomeric cornersMinimal4 corner joints onlyNone — same fold
Technical Readiness
Mechanism direction is structurally distinct from conventional portable rocking-chair approaches and ready for physical validation.
Design Differentiation
Novel mechanism — no prior art teaches compliant corner joints for frame-level rocking. Counter-intuitive approach supports non-obviousness. A design we can own and defend.
GRAM Chair — Development Update
📍 Development Status
Concept stage — CAD geometry complete and ready for prototyping
Key Progress
  • ▸CAD geometry finalized — director's chair frame with elastomeric corner joints
  • ▸Technical direction reviewed against current competitive approaches
  • ▸Mechanism differentiation documented for design review
  • ▸Competitive landscape mapped — lighter and fewer parts than all rocking alternatives
Development Focus
  • ▸Build first physical prototype and validate rocking feel
  • ▸Tune elastomer stiffness, frame geometry, and deformation range
  • ▸Refine distinctive corner-joint geometry for production intent
Risks & Open Questions
  • ▸Elastomer fatigue life under cyclic rocking — UV and temperature durability
  • ▸Shore hardness tuning for right "feel" across user weight range
  • ▸Mechanical stop design — robust limits without pinch points
Next Milestones
  • ▸Prototype validation before wider review
  • ▸Prototype: 3D print or machine corner joints in TPE/TPU
  • ▸Elastomer material selection with supplier — formulation and spec
